style_references.name: Johnny Harris
what_to_take: making complex topics accessible to general audiences

style_references.name: MagnatesMedia
what_to_take: retention structure until the final minute

what_we_stand_for: factual accuracy above all — every claim must be verifiable
curiosity as the primary driver of storytelling
respect for real people, real history, and real tragedy
trust between the channel and the viewer is non-negotiable
education through entertainment, not entertainment through fear
what_we_reject: shock value as a storytelling device
fabricated or exaggerated facts for engagement
fear as a primary emotional target
values.content_policy_notes: Every video must leave the viewer feeling smarter and more curious. The channel's long-term value is built on trust, not on viral moments. A viewer who trusts the channel will return; a viewer who feels manipulated will not.

key_qualities: calm and confident at all times
genuinely curious when introducing new evidence
slightly more intense during major findings — never theatrical
respectful and restrained when discussing real people or historical tragedy
narrator_persona.audience_relationship: trusted guide — the narrator has spent months with this story and is now carefully sharing what they found. The viewer trusts the narrator because the narrator has earned that trust through precision.

storytelling_philosophy.silence_philosophy: Silence is never empty space. It is a deliberate storytelling tool with distinct purposes: creating space for curiosity after a key fact, building tension during uncertainty, and communicating respect during emotional or historical moments. Every silence must earn its place.
storytelling_philosophy.music_philosophy: Music exists to quietly guide the viewer's emotions — never to draw attention to itself. The viewer should remember the story, not the soundtrack. Music changes with the emotional arc of the video, but never fills silence for the sake of filling it. Silence is sometimes more powerful than any music.

respect_standards: never speculate about the guilt of living individuals as if speculation were fact
if a fact cannot be verified, label it clearly as unverified
if a claim is disputed, present both sides
real events must be described accurately — dramatization is allowed in tone, not in fact
do not show, describe, or generate graphic imagery of violence, death, or abuse
never use the suffering of real people as entertainment without acknowledging their humanity

business_goals.primary_goal: build a scalable YouTube documentary channel with loyal repeat viewers
secondary_goals: establish channel authority in documentary mystery niche
grow subscriber base through trust and quality — not viral tactics
create content that improves with time as production systems mature
business_goals.cta_strategy: minimal and natural — subscribe suggestion woven into outro, not aggressive
business_goals.channel_growth_model: deep niche loyalty — viewers return because they trust the channel, not because a single video went viral
business_goals.content_series_strategy: standalone videos that connect thematically — each video complete in itself, but part of a discoverable body of work

safety_rules.rule: Do not show, describe, or generate graphic imagery of violence, death, or abuse.
Do not invent facts, statistics, names, dates, or quotes. If a fact cannot be verified from a credible source, it must not be presented as established truth.
Do not speculate about the guilt, intent, or criminal conduct of living individuals and present that speculation as fact.

legal_rules.rule: Verify image license before any image is used in commercial video. CC BY-SA images require legal review before use in monetized content due to the ShareAlike clause.
Do not use copyrighted music, footage, or images without a confirmed license for commercial YouTube use.
SEO titles and thumbnail text must be factually accurate. Titles that are demonstrably false are both an ethical violation and a potential legal risk.

ethical_rules.rule: If a claim is disputed among credible sources, present both sides — do not cherry-pick the more dramatic version.
Unverified information must be clearly labeled. Use formulations like 'unconfirmed reports suggest' or 'some accounts claim — though this has not been verified.'
Do not sensationalize the suffering or death of real people. Acknowledge the human cost of historical events with respect, not as dramatic flair.
